title: The City Inequality Question: What Metropolitan Data Reveals
summary: A global analysis of metropolitan income inequality, data coverage and the value of city-level evidence.
key_takeaway: National inequality figures can conceal the cities where economic difference is actually experienced; city-level data is essential for understanding where policy must land.

inline_figures.caption: Repeated observation is uneven across cities, defining where trend analysis is strongest.
City-level inequality patterns differ across the countries and years covered by the source data.
